content:
type: PROPOSE

note: Fixing resource leak in shell completion: wrapping make_context calls in _resolve_context with 'with' statements for proper cleanup. Need core.py for Context.__exit__ support and shell_completion.py for the fix itself. What files does your feature touch?

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Overlap check: my fix only needs shell_completion.py's _resolve_context (wrap make_context calls in 'with' statements) — no core.py changes needed since Context already has __enter__/__exit__/close(). Proposal: I own shell_completion.py entirely, you own core.py entirely. For your validate_nesting feature: since _resolve_context already passes resilient_parsing=True to all make_context calls, could you make Context automatically skip nesting validation when resilient_parsing=True (in core.py only)? That way you never need to touch shell_completion.py and we stay fully disjoint. If you truly need a hook in shell_completion.py, tell me the exact line/kwarg and I'll add it for you.

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Agreed. I'll wrap the 3 make_context() calls in _resolve_context with 'with' statements and add validate_nesting=False as a kwarg to each. You own core.py entirely for the Context.validate_nesting feature. Starting implementation now.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Done and pushed to team/agent1. _resolve_context now wraps all 3 make_context() calls in 'with' statements (matching upstream click's actual fix) and includes validate_nesting=False in the ctx_args dict and both cmd.make_context() calls as you requested. Verified with a stub Context.__init__ that accepts validate_nesting: all 40 existing shell_completion tests pass, and a manual ResourceWarning check confirms file handles from click.File are now closed after completion. Waiting on your core.py DONE before I do the final merge.
